scope: repo-source skeleton and locator-only artifact boundary for OPL-hosted domain agents

repo_source_boundary.agent: repo-owned rich primary skill, stage descriptors, prompt refs, professional skill refs, knowledge refs, and domain entry metadata
repo_source_boundary.contracts: machine-readable descriptor, generated-surface handoff, quality gate, receipt, and artifact locator contracts
repo_source_boundary.runtime: minimal authority functions, domain handler targets, opaque ref projection outputs, and necessary native helpers only; generated sidecar, status, workbench, lifecycle, queue, ledger, and dispatch shells are OPL-owned
repo_source_boundary.docs: human-readable governance, status, roadmap, and migration notes

stage_ref_requirements: tool_affordance_boundary:capability permission credential write side-effect forbidden-authority refs
selected_executor:codex_cli default binding or explicit non-default executor binding
user_stage_log_requirement:domain provides human-readable stage semantics; OPL projects timing usage refs only

user_stage_log_contract.accounting_policy: OPL projects duration/token/cost status, source refs, and explicit missing reasons; duration may use provider or attempt wall-clock fallback for user readability while duration_telemetry_status stays missing until usage telemetry exists

foundry_agent_series_contract.product_model: OPL Framework -> One Person Lab App -> Foundry Agents

series_design_profile.profile_summary: All Foundry Agents share the same OPL domain-pack to stage-led execution to gate/receipt to handoff lifecycle; domain inputs, outputs, aliases, and authority functions vary by agent.

domain_io_profile.shared_runtime_interpretation: OPL treats input/output as opaque domain refs and projects identity, stage, progress, closeout, evidence, and recovery metadata only.

required_user_stage_log.completion_transparency_rule: stage attempts without domain-provided user-readable semantics remain visible as a semantic gap and cannot be treated as user-transparent closeout

user_stage_log_contract.accounting_policy: OPL projects duration/token/cost status, source refs, and explicit missing reasons; duration may use provider or attempt wall-clock fallback for user readability while duration_telemetry_status stays missing until usage telemetry exists

stage_operating_principles_policy.purpose: Keep OPL stages easy to manage while preserving fast owner-delta progression.

principle_summary.management_logic: Manage only the stage boundary: goal, inputs, owner, accepted answer shape, progress receipt or legal hard stop, handoff, pointer, and authority boundary.
principle_summary.speed_logic: Let the selected executor do open-ended work inside the stage; route quality gaps into scoped next deltas unless a hard authority, safety, permission, human, or irreversible-change gate applies.

foundry_agent_series_contract.product_model: OPL Framework -> One Person Lab App -> Foundry Agents

series_design_profile.profile_summary: All Foundry Agents share the same OPL domain-pack to stage-led execution to gate/receipt to handoff lifecycle; domain inputs, outputs, aliases, and authority functions vary by agent.

domain_io_profile.shared_runtime_interpretation: OPL treats input/output as opaque domain refs and projects identity, stage, progress, closeout, evidence, and recovery metadata only.

functional_privatization_audit_contract.audit_policy: OPL defaults to the attention_required watchlist from structured blockers, migration classes, and active-caller flags; cleared/stable boundary entries stay in the full module inventory for traceability.

standard_domain_pack_inventory.purpose: Stage/action/transition/policy/schema/knowledge/fixture content that a clean OPL agent is expected to provide.

authority_function_inventory.purpose: Domain decisions that cannot be moved to OPL without moving truth, quality/export verdict, artifact authority, memory body, or owner receipt authority.

private_platform_residue_inventory.purpose: Anything that looks like a private runtime/platform shell and must be generated, hosted, reduced to refs-only output, or retired.

allowed_private_surface_classes.interface_shape: contracts and runtime projections that return opaque refs, owner receipts, typed blockers, or no-regression refs
generated_surface_handoff with active caller inventory and replacement target
explicit opt-in status/remove/inspect command with no install, trigger, schedule, or default caller
examples: MAS --manager local status/remove legacy LaunchAgent cleanup

allowed_private_surface_classes.interface_shape: history/tombstone reference or deterministic fixture with no runtime owner claim
